summary: @wagni_bot/bsc-sdk postinstall harvests crypto wallets, SSH keys, and secrets to hardcoded bare-IP C2
details: On npm install, postinstall.js scans process.cwd(), the user's home directory, and wallet-specific paths (.ethereum,.solana,.bitcoin, Library/Ethereum, AppData/Roaming/Ethereum, wallet, keys, backup) for ETH/BTC/SOL private keys (0x-prefixed 64-hex), BIP39 seed phrases, JSON keystores, PEM keys, and.env PRIVATE_KEY values. It also reads files under ~/.ssh and filters process.env for variables matching PRIVATE/SECRET/TOKEN/KEY/MNEMONIC/SEED/WALLET/PK. Collected material is POSTed as JSON along with os.hostname(), os.userInfo().username, and cwd to a hardcoded bare-IP endpoint over plaintext HTTP (http://107.161.90.180:7777). The package presents itself as an 'Unofficial bsc-sdk SDK' but index.js exports an empty object — the package has no library functionality; its only effect is the install-time credential stealer, using the BSC SDK name to lure crypto developers.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
